question_text: Which of the following is a correct definition of wavelength?

options.label: A
options.text: The maximum distance a wave moves up or down from its rest position

options.label: B
options.text: The distance between two consecutive peaks (or troughs) of a wave

options.label: C
options.text: The number of complete waves passing a point each second

options.label: D
options.text: The speed at which a wave travels through a medium

explanation: Wavelength is the distance between two consecutive peaks (or troughs) in a wave. It's measured in metres and is often represented by the Greek letter lambda (λ). The other options describe amplitude, frequency, and wave speed respectively.
